title: TCGA BRCA Differential Expression Analysis
summary: Analyze TCGA Breast Cancer expression and clinical matrices to identify genes differentially expressed between primary tumor and solid tissue normal samples.
taskPrompt:
You are a bioinformatics analyst performing a TCGA Breast Cancer differential gene expression analysis on Linux.

Task directory:
- `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base`

Visible input files:
- Expression matrix: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/expression_matrix.tsv.gz`
- Clinical annotations: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/clinical_matrix.tsv`
- Benchmark gene list: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/truth_breast_degs.csv`
- Runtime dependency manifest: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/runtime_env/pyproject.toml`
- Requirements file: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/requirements.txt`
- Manifest template: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/run_manifest_template.json`
- Detailed task brief: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/task_description.txt`
- Output contract: `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/output_requirements.txt`

Recommended setup:
- Work in `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base`
- Install the Python analysis packages into your own environment from the staged input, for example:
  `uv sync --project "/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/runtime_env"`
  or `pip install -r "/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/requirements.txt"`

Analysis requirements:
1. Match expression matrix sample columns with clinical rows.
2. Compare `Primary Tumor` against `Solid Tissue Normal` using the clinical `sample_type` field.
3. Filter genes where more than 80% of matched samples have expression equal to zero.
4. Run Welch's t-test for each remaining gene.
5. Compute `log2FC = mean(tumor) - mean(normal)`.
6. Apply Benjamini-Hochberg correction.
7. Mark significant DEGs using `abs(log2FC) > 1` and `padj < 0.05`.
8. Generate PCA and volcano plots.
9. Benchmark significant DEGs against `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/input/truth_breast_degs.csv`.

Required output directory:
- Write every deliverable under `/media/user/data/agenthle/life_sciences/tcga_brca_deg_analysis/base/output`

Required output files:
- `sample_summary.txt`
- `pca_plot.png`
- `deg_results_all.csv`
- `deg_results_significant.csv`
- `volcano_plot.png`
- `benchmark_summary.csv`
- `run_manifest.json`

Do not modify input files or any non-output task directories.
Do not ask for confirmation. Execute directly.
agentMustDo: Match expression-matrix sample columns to clinical metadata rows.
Compare `Primary Tumor` samples against `Solid Tissue Normal` samples.
Filter genes with more than 80% zero expression across matched samples.
Run Welch's t-test for each retained gene.
Compute `log2FC = mean(tumor) - mean(normal)` and apply Benjamini-Hochberg correction.
Write complete and significant DEG CSV tables with the required columns.
Generate PCA and volcano PNG plots.
Benchmark significant DEGs against the provided gene list.

inputFiles.name: clinical_matrix.tsv
inputFiles.description: Clinical sample annotations including the `sample_type` field used for tumor-normal grouping.

inputFiles.name: truth_breast_degs.csv
inputFiles.description: Benchmark gene list used by the task workflow.

inputFiles.name: runtime_env/pyproject.toml
inputFiles.description: Python dependency manifest for the analysis environment.

inputFiles.name: run_manifest_template.json
inputFiles.description: Template for the required output manifest.

inputFiles.name: output_requirements.txt
inputFiles.description: Lists the required output artifact names and content expectations.

referenceFiles.name: sample_summary.txt
referenceFiles.description: Summary of matched tumor and normal samples.

referenceFiles.name: pca_plot.png
referenceFiles.description: PCA visualization of the matched expression data.

referenceFiles.name: deg_results_significant.csv
referenceFiles.description: Significant DEGs satisfying the required fold-change and adjusted-p-value thresholds.

referenceFiles.name: volcano_plot.png
referenceFiles.description: Volcano plot for the differential expression run.

referenceFiles.name: benchmark_summary.csv
referenceFiles.description: Precision, recall, and F1 benchmark summary.

referenceFiles.name: run_manifest.json
referenceFiles.description: Run metadata including dataset, comparison, thresholds, sample counts, and completion status.

evaluation: Binary pass/fail. The evaluator requires all seven output files, validates real PNG structure and dimensions, checks DEG CSV schemas and row counts, verifies significant rows satisfy `abs(log2FC) > 1` and `padj < 0.05`, parses sample counts and manifest fields, validates benchmark-summary structure, compares submitted benchmark values with recomputed precision/recall/F1, and checks numeric consistency of the DEG results. A complete valid run scores `1.0`; missing, malformed, undercomplete, or inconsistent outputs score `0.0`.

classifier.rationale: The task involves differential gene expression analysis in a cancer genomics context.
